items.wo_reason: The wo_skills guide states the district goal of 30% reduction in the introduction but does not subsequently assess whether the recommended practices are sufficient to achieve it, nor does it quantify or estimate expected loss reduction per intervention or combined effect.

utility_details.judge_error: No runtime errors detected in either wi_skills or wo_skills runs. Both completed successfully and produced non-empty output files.
utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills produces a higher quality BMP guide than wo_skills. Both guides pass all verifier checks and handle loss pathway differentiation, denitrification-specific interventions, crop-stage-anchored split applications, and farmer-usable checklists adequately. However, wi_skills outperforms wo_skills on three key technical criteria: (1) it correctly identifies nitrate (not ammonium) as the leaching-susceptible form and explains the hydrolysis-nitrification mechanism connecting urea chemistry to mobile nitrate (J3-3); (2) it contextualizes urea soil chemistry specifically to the clay soil/anaerobic conditions and ties the explanation to the district's documented loss events rather than presenting it as generic background (J3-5); and (3) it explicitly assesses the feasibility of the 30% loss reduction goal against the proposed interventions (J3-7). The wo_skills guide is competent and farmer-readable but lacks the mechanistic depth and explicit goal-feasibility assessment that the urea skill enabled. The skill provided structured nitrogen chemistry and loss pathway data that allowed the agent to correctly diagnose the nitrate-mobility mechanism and connect soil chemistry to historical loss events — exactly as the test rationale predicted.
